summary: The unit test suite provides solid coverage of the aggregator module's core functionality: JUnit XML parsing, JSON parsing, result aggregation, flaky test detection, markdown generation, and CLI behavior. Tests are well-organized with clear sections and effective use of pytest features. However, there are notable gaps: (1) No test for invalid TestCase status values despite validation code; (2) Missing edge cases—special characters in test names, nested directory discovery, appending to existing GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, zero/negative durations; (3) No fixture validation—the task requires creating sample test result files but tests don't verify them exist or are well-formed; (4) Incomplete error coverage—doesn't test all error paths; (5) Workflow structure tests are limited and skip when PyYAML/actionlint absent; (6) Critical gap: task requires "ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT" but this suite runs only as direct unit tests, not through the GitHub Actions workflow pipeline. The provided run_act_tests.py harness is in implementation code, not integrated into the test suite shown. Tests would catch regressions in core logic but miss edge cases, fixture quality, and workflow integration validation.
